company.description: Acting Office is a next-generation enterprise software platform designed specifically for medium and large sized accounting firms. It provides a fully integrated software platform for modern accountancy practices that want precision, efficiency and complete visibility across their operations.

news.title: Acting Office
news.summary:
Benefits of integrating Acting Office The Acting Office integration with FreeAgent will bring significant efficiency and accuracy benefits by connecting the practice software directly with the bookkeeping data.
All financial information for your clients in FreeAgent is instantly available in Acting Office.
Trial balances can be mapped and imported directly from FreeAgent, and Acting Office automatically reads and interprets FreeAgent account codes for faster processing.
How Acting Office works with FreeAgent The integration with FreeAgent enables the seamless transfer of client data and financial records directly into Acting Office.
How do I get started with Acting Office?

Run your entire firm from a single client record.

Book a demo

news.title: What is acting officer? Simple Definition & Meaning · LSD.Law
news.summary:
To ensure continuous operation and leadership, the Governor appoints the Deputy Director to serve as the acting Director while a search for a new permanent director is conducted.
This illustrates an acting officer stepping into a vacant position to maintain essential government services.
Example 2 (Corporate Context): The Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of a technology company takes an approved three-month sabbatical.
The Board of Directors designates the Chief Operating Officer (COO) to serve as the acting CEO during this period.
This demonstrates an acting officer taking over due to the incapacitation of the permanent office holder.

news.title: Acting (law)
news.summary:
For the law governing people acting on someone else's behalf, see law of agency.
In law, a person is acting in a position if they are not serving in the position on a permanent basis.
[1] Politics [edit] Examples of acting positions in politics include acting mayor, acting governor, acting president, and acting prime minister.
Being placed in an acting position is a good indicator that the acting person has the confidence of their superiors or colleagues, and is likely to be chosen for the position on a permanent basis.
[6] The rules for appointment of acting officials are covered in many cases by the Federal Vacancies Reform Act of 1998 (FVRA).

ai_insights.summary: Acting Office is a growing enterprise software company in the accounting technology space, offering an AI-powered platform that integrates various accounting functions into a single system. The company has demonstrated success with clients like Caroola, showing significant efficiency improvements.
